accepted.statement: A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the Sun
Neptune has winds that travel faster than the speed of sound on Earth
It rains diamonds on Neptune and Uranus
A neutron star is so dense that a teaspoon of its material would weigh about a billion tonnes on Earth
If you stretched out all the DNA in a single human body, it would reach from Earth to Pluto and back — multiple times
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire
The shortest war in recorded history lasted only 38 to 45 minutes — between Britain and Zanzibar in 1896
The Great Wall of China is NOT visible from space with the naked eye
Napoleon Bonaparte was not particularly short — he was about average height for his time
The Library of Alexandria may have been destroyed gradually over centuries — not in a single dramatic fire
Russia is so wide it spans 11 time zones — more than any other country on Earth.
The Amazon River discharges more freshwater into the ocean than the next seven largest rivers combined.
Mount Everest is the tallest mountain above sea level, but Mauna Kea in Hawaii is the tallest mountain measured from its base.
The Sahara Desert is roughly the same size as the contiguous United States.
The first computer bug was a literal insect — a moth found trapped in a relay of the Harvard Mark II computer in 1947.
A single strand of fiber optic cable can carry more than 1 petabit of data per second — enough to stream every film ever made in under a minute.
The Apollo 11 guidance computer that landed humans on the Moon had 4 KB of RAM — about 100,000 times less memory than a modern smartphone.
The internet transmits approximately 5 exabytes of data every single day — more data than was created in all of human history up to the year 2003.
Velcro was invented in 1941 after Swiss engineer George de Mestral returned from a hike and noticed burdock burrs stuck to his dog's fur.
The Mona Lisa has no eyebrows — Renaissance Florentine fashion dictated that shaved eyebrows were the height of elegance.
Giant forest genets are small carnivorous mammals found in African forests.
Parnassius apollo is a butterfly species found in European mountain regions.
Waffles originated in France as a batter-based food cooked between patterned plates.
Labskaus is a hearty culinary specialty from Northern Germany and Scandinavia.
Mille-feuille is a French pastry with layers of puff pastry and cream.
Crêpes are very thin pancakes that originated in France.
Cupid is the Roman god of love, counterpart to Greek god Eros.
Curupira is a mythological forest guardian creature from Brazilian folklore.
The Loch Ness Monster is a legendary creature from Scottish folklore inhabiting Loch Ness.
The Easter Bunny is a folkloric figure symbolizing Easter and springtime renewal.
Kubikajiri is a Japanese yokai that bites off human heads from behind.
Advanced Photovoltaic Experiment tested solar panel efficiency in orbital space.
A-101 was an early German rocket test flight program.
A-103 was a Soviet test mission exploring spacecraft systems.
Apollo A-104 was an unmanned orbital test of Apollo spacecraft in 1965.
A-105 was the final boilerplate test of an Apollo spacecraft.

rejected.statement: There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than there are atoms in the observable universe
Tardigrades can survive the vacuum of space, intense radiation, and temperatures from near absolute zero to 150°C
Honey never spoils — archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible
Trees in a forest communicate and share nutrients through underground fungal networks nicknamed the 'Wood Wide Web'
The mantis shrimp can punch with the acceleration of a bullet and sees colours humans cannot imagine
The Great Wall of China is NOT visible from space with the naked eye — it's a persistent myth.
Beethoven composed his Ninth Symphony — including the famous 'Ode to Joy' — after he was completely deaf.
The ancient Greeks believed music was so powerful that different musical modes could literally change a person's moral character.
The Norse god Odin sacrificed one of his eyes to gain wisdom, then hung himself from a tree for nine days to learn the secret of writing.

Japan's 'Osechi Ryori' New Year food tradition assigns specific meanings to every dish — black beans represent health, herring roe represents fertility, and shrimp represent longevity because they curl like an old person's back.
Senna alata is a tropical plant species with least concern conservation status.
Leopard cats are small wild felines native to Asia with spotted coats.
Phaseolus vulgaris is a legume crop cultivated worldwide for food.
The hamburger is an American sandwich made with a ground beef patty.
